evidence: Investing in innovation to accelerate organic growth is a critical component of Emerson's value creation strategy. These initiatives include disruptive measurement technologies, software-defined automation systems, self-optimizing asset software.
Our businesses increasingly rely on artificial intelligence solutions to optimize our operations, improve customer experiences, and enhance our products and services.
Total spending for R&D, engineering expense and customer-funded engineering and development was 8.1 percent of sales in 2025 and in 2024, compared to 6.9 percent in 2023.
Our businesses increasingly rely on artificial intelligence solutions to optimize our operations, improve customer experiences, and enhance our products and services.
Our businesses are affected by varying degrees of technological change, such as, among others, artificial intelligences and machine learning, and corresponding shifts in customer demand.
These initiatives include disruptive measurement technologies, software-defined automation systems, self-optimizing asset software and sustainability solutions.

We anticipate that the risk of cybersecurity attacks will increase as artificial intelligence capabilities improve...with the possibility of additional vulnerabilities being introduced through our own use of artificial intelligence.

Our artificial intelligence efforts subject us to risks related to accuracy, intellectual property infringement or misappropriation, data privacy, and cybersecurity...if our use of artificial intelligence becomes controversial, we may experience brand or reputational harm, competitive harm, or legal liability.

The regulatory landscape surrounding artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving and the use of artificial intelligence may be subject to new legal or regulatory requirements, the impact of which may be prohibitive or pose further risks.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: R&D spend of 8.1% of sales is disclosed but not AI-attributable specifically; no dollar amount or headcount tied explicitly to AI investment. Named AI initiatives exist but lack quantification, capping score at 3.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use cases named (operations optimization, customer experience, product enhancement) but no deployment metrics, quantified outcomes, or production-status detail provided in excerpts.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk section material is present and discusses AI risks with named categories and mechanisms (cybersecurity, IP, data privacy, regulatory, competitive harm), qualifying for score 4; no quantification or named governance body found to reach score 5.
capex_mentions: No AI-attributable dollar capex figures found in excerpts; total capex figures present but not linked to AI.
